issues.title: A KeyRepeatEvent is dispatched, but the state shows that the physical key is not pressed
issues.body:
# Edited by Moderator

Most users report that this issue exist only on v3.3.x or earlier.

**Please only report if this error exists on v3.4.0 or later**. 

When reporting, **please make sure** to add your `flutter doctor -v` result as well as reproduction steps. Thank you.

------


<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. In Windows 10, Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Click in the app
3. Hold down the left control key
4. While holding it, click or move the mouse - see the error


**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
Want the logged bug not to happen

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
```
======== Exception caught by services library ======================================================
The following assertion was thrown during a platform message callback:
A KeyRepeatEvent is dispatched, but the state shows that the physical key is not pressed. If this occurs in real application, please report this bug to Flutter. If this occurs in unit tests, please ensure that simulated events follow Flutter's event model as documented in `HardwareKeyboard`. This was the event: KeyRepeatEvent#bae6a(physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey#700e0(usbHidUsage: "0x000700e0", debugName: "Control Left"), logicalKey: LogicalKeyboardKey#00100(keyId: "0x200000100", keyLabel: "Control Left", debugName: "Control Left"), character: null, timeStamp: 50:55:02.589706)
'package:flutter/src/services/hardware_keyboard.dart':
Failed assertion: line 432 pos 16: '_pressedKeys.containsKey(event.physicalKey)'


Either the assertion indicates an error in the framework itself, or we should provide substantially more information in this error message to help you determine and fix the underlying cause.
In either case, please report this assertion by filing a bug on GitHub:
  https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/new?template=2_bug.md

When the exception was thrown, this was the stack: 
#2      HardwareKeyboard._assertEventIsRegular.<anonymous closure> (package:flutter/src/services/hardware_keyboard.dart:432:16)
```

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/rendering.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({Key? key, required this.title}) : super(key: key);

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class UndoIntent extends Intent {
  const UndoIntent();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  FocusNode myFocusNode = new FocusNode();

  Widget wrapFocus(Widget child) {
    print("Wrapping focus");
    return Focus(
        child: child,
        onFocusChange: (focused) {
          print("Focus changed to $focused");
        },
        // focusNode: myFocusNode
    );
  }

  Widget buildViewport(BuildContext context) {
    final Map<ShortcutActivator, Intent> activators;
    activators = <ShortcutActivator, Intent>{
      const SingleActivator(LogicalKeyboardKey.keyZ, control: true): const UndoIntent(),
    };

    return Shortcuts(
        shortcuts: activators,
        child: wrapFocus(Actions(actions: <Type, Action<Intent>>{
          UndoIntent: CallbackAction<UndoIntent>(
            onInvoke: (UndoIntent intent) =>
                setState(() {
                  print("Undo action");
                }),
          ),
        },
          child: Container(
            child: CustomPaint(
              size: const Size(100, 100),
            painter: MyPainter(context),
          ),
        ))));
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: Column(
        children: [
          Container(width: 20, height:50, color: Colors.red),
          buildViewport(context),
        ]
      ),
    );
  }
}

class MyPainter extends CustomPainter {
  BuildContext context;
  MyPainter(this.context);

  @override
  bool shouldRepaint(CustomPainter oldDelegate) {
    return true;
  }

  @override
  void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
    var text = '$size';
    TextSpan span = TextSpan(style: const TextStyle(color: Colors.black, fontSize: 12.0, fontFamily: 'Roboto'), text: text);
    TextPainter tp = TextPainter(text: span, textDirection: TextDirection.ltr, textAlign: TextAlign.center);
    tp.layout();
    var pt = Offset(150, 150);
    tp.paint(canvas, Offset(pt.dx - tp.width / 2, pt.dy - tp.height / 2));


    // diagonal line
    canvas.drawLine(Offset.zero, Offset(size.width, size.height), Paint()..color = Colors.black);
  }
}
```

Then select some text in the Flutter application, and a toolbar will be displayed at the mouse position.
